title: Angelica trifoliolata
intro: Angelica trifoliolata, also known as three-leafed angelica or Korean angelica, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Angelica trifoliolata include volatile oils, sesquiterpenes, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Angelica trifoliolata is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Angelica trifoliolata is a member of the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification suggests that Angelica trifoliolata is closely related to other plants in the same family. Within the Apiaceae family, it is classified as a species within the genus Angelica. The genus Angelica is further classified as being part of the order Apiales. The plant's classification indicates its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
uses: Angelica trifoliolata, a plant native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Furthermore, the plant is believed to possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds. Additionally, the plant is reported to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.

parts: Angelica trifoliolata, also known as three-leaf angelica, is a plant species used in traditional medicine.
